text: 34g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
6.2g of carbs. sugar, glycemic index, and starch are doing most of the work. for a dairy food, this is doing the right stuff.
6.2g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
vitamin b12 at 10% daily value, vitamin a at 22% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
calcium at 10% daily value, zinc at 4% daily value, magnesium at 2% daily value stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

the upsides first: easy protein delivery for normal diets because dairy gets real credit for convenience. clear calcium identity because that is a core category strength. slots into meals very easily because practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

on the weaker side: not everyone tolerates dairy well because that is a real use-case limitation. fat quality varies wildly across dairy because some dairy is clean, some really is not. processing changes the category a lot because the label alone does not tell the whole story.
C tier. Best only when the taste or format matters more than getting the strongest dairy profile.
